name: Goll mac Morna
description: Goll mac Morna, also known as Conán Maol, was a prominent warrior in the Fianna, the legendary band of Irish heroes. He is often portrayed as a fierce and formidable figure, renowned for his strength and skill in battle, though sometimes depicted with a brutish nature. Goll is known for his pivotal role in the events leading to the rise of Fionn mac Cumhaill as leader of the Fianna. He killed Fionn's father, Cumhall, in battle, initially becoming the leader of the Fianna himself. However, he later relinquished his position to Fionn after the young hero demonstrated exceptional bravery and wisdom. Despite their initial animosity, Goll eventually became a loyal and valuable member of Fionn's band, participating in numerous adventures and battles. His most notable physical characteristic was that he only had one eye, having lost the other in a childhood incident or, in some versions, during a fight. He is a complex character, embodying both heroic and antagonistic traits, and is a significant figure in the Fenian Cycle of Irish mythology.
subtitle: One-eyed warrior of the Fianna.
achievements: Remaining a loyal member of the Fianna

enemies.name: Various monsters and enemies of the Fianna

keyMyths.title: The Killing of Cumhall
keyMyths.description: Goll mac Morna killed Cumhall, Fionn mac Cumhaill's father, in battle, leading to a lifelong connection between Goll and Fionn. This act set in motion the events that would eventually lead to Fionn becoming the leader of the Fianna.

keyMyths.title: Goll's Leadership of the Fianna
keyMyths.description: After killing Cumhall, Goll briefly led the Fianna before relinquishing the position to Fionn. He recognized Fionn's inherent leadership qualities and the potential for greatness within him.

keyMyths.title: Goll's Loyalty to Fionn
keyMyths.description: Despite their initially antagonistic relationship, Goll became a loyal and valuable member of Fionn's Fianna. He fought alongside Fionn in numerous battles and adventures, proving his worth as a warrior.

sources.author: Lady Gregory
sources.description: A collection of Irish myths and legends.
sources.text: A collection of Irish myths and legends.
